title: phyllachne clavigera
intro: Phyllachne clavigera, also known as the club moss, is a type of plant that belongs to the club moss family and is native to New Zealand. Traditionally, Phyllachne clavigera has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system. Phyllachne clavigera can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
